page.content: The analysis of the tables reveals significant data across varied parameters. In Table 1, under the header "Artifact Reduction Method," the "Reader Assignment of Seizure-Onset Laterality" and the "Percentage of reader assignments in concordance with SOZ laterality defined by other clinical criteria" are detailed, with values for AR1 showing Right at 59.3 (28.5-84.2) (n=1), Unknown at 66.8 (38.1-86.9) (n=3), and Left at 91.9 (77.0-97.5) (n=4); AR2 presents Right at 61.8 (31.3-85.1) (n=1), Unknown at 71.4 (42.8-89.3) (n=3), and Left at 95.9 (85.7-98.9) (n=4). In Table 2, the "Stores" category showcases estimates and p-values across two models: Model 1 (with summer peak) and Model 2 (with ARIMA-specified seasonality), where the Baseline level is 3105.1 (192.6) with a p-value of <0.0001 for both models; the Policy shows −353.60 (413.00) with a p-value of 0.39 in Model 1 and −663.10 (439.50) with a p-value of 0.13 in Model 2; the Summer peak estimate is 533.70 (205.50) with a p-value of 0.01 in Model 1, while Model 2 has no estimate provided.

Method," the "Reader Assignment of Seizure-Onset Laterality" and the "Percentage of reader assignments in concordance with
SOZ laterality defined by other clinical criteria" are detailed, with values for AR1 showing Right at 59.3 (28.5-84.2) (n=1),
Unknown at 66.8 (38.1-86.9) (n=3), and Left at 91.9 (77.0-97.5) (n=4); AR2 presents Right at 61.8 (31.3-85.1) (n=1), Unknown
at 71.4 (42.8-89.3) (n=3), and Left at 95.9 (85.7-98.9) (n=4). In Table 2, the "Stores" category showcases estimates and p-values
across two models: Model 1 (with summer peak) and Model 2 (with ARIMA-specified seasonality), where the Baseline level is
3105.1 (192.6) with a p-value of <0.0001 for both models; the Policy shows −353.60 (413.00) with a p-value of 0.39 in Model 1
and −663.10 (439.50) with a p-value of 0.13 in Model 2; the Summer peak estimate is 533.70 (205.50) with a p-value of 0.01 in
Model 1, while Model 2 has no estimate provided.
